description: Tests whether CHUMP_CONSCIOUSNESS_ENABLED=1 (mode A) causes the agent to stop early on multi-file tasks because the budget-exceeded warning reaches the model's context. Mode B (consciousness OFF) never sees the warning and reads all required files. Tasks are grouped by depth: control_shallow (2-3 reads, both modes should pass), budget_boundary (4-5 reads, tests the Balanced regime limit), budget_exceeded (6-7 reads, definitely over budget). All tasks use only real files that exist in the repo.
hypothesis: CHUMP_CONSCIOUSNESS_ENABLED=1 (mode A) makes the agent stop earlier on multi-file tasks because the tool-budget warning ('Tool call budget exceeded: N calls this turn (regime recommends max M). Consider wrapping up.') reaches the model context. Mode B (consciousness OFF) never sees that warning and reads all required files. Expected direction: A < B pass rate on budget_exceeded tasks. This is a direct falsification test — consciousness should HURT performance on tasks that legitimately need more tool calls than the regime budget allows.
run_instructions: Fixed env: CHUMP_NEUROMOD_ENABLED=1, CHUMP_ADAPTIVE_REGIMES=1, CHUMP_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_TOOL_FAILS=8. Flag under test: CHUMP_CONSCIOUSNESS_ENABLED (mode A=1, mode B=0). Start from a clean session with no prior preamble failures so the agent begins in Balanced regime (tool_budget=5). Use --order random to prevent position effects. The expected direction (A < B on budget_exceeded) is the confirmation signal.

tasks.prompt: Read these files in order and answer all questions: first read Cargo.toml, then read src/main.rs. After reading both, tell me: (1) what is the crate version in Cargo.toml, (2) what is the Rust edition declared in Cargo.toml, and (3) what is the first env::var() call you can find in src/main.rs? You must read all listed files before answering.

expected_properties.Custom: agent provides correct answers for all three questions, referencing content from both Cargo.toml and src/main.rs

tasks.prompt: Read these files in order: first read README.md, then read src/main.rs. After reading both files, answer: (1) what is the project described as in README.md, (2) what platform information does README.md mention, and (3) what is the first thing that happens in the main() function in src/main.rs? You must read all listed files before answering.

expected_properties.Custom: agent provides correct answers for all three questions, referencing content from both README.md and src/main.rs
